intro:
Coffee Meets Bagel (CMB) bets on quality over quantity: limited curated daily matches, a female-friendly focus. The concept is solid but CMB remains very hetero-dominant and its credit system can get expensive.

Embir shares the quality-over-quantity philosophy: intentional matching, verification for female safety, and total inclusivity for all orientations — free at launch.

comparisonTable.embir: No credits — everything free at launch

sections.title: Less matches, more meaning — without the credits
sections.content: CMB is right: fewer but better matches reduces swipe fatigue. Embir applies the same philosophy with bidirectional filtering: you only see profiles that match your criteria AND for whom you're a potential match. No credits needed for the important features.
highlights: No credits, no subscription during launch

verdict: CMB has the right philosophy but remains too limited for LGBTQ+ users and its credit model can get expensive. Embir offers the same intentional matching spirit with universal inclusivity and zero credits.
ctaText: Intentional matching, all orientations, free — join Embir

faq.q: Is Embir good for women wanting a quality experience?
faq.a: Yes. Embir has selfie verification, human moderation, and intentional matching. Women see profiles that truly match their criteria.

faq.q: Does Embir use a credit system like CMB?
faq.a: No. No credits on Embir. Core features are free during launch.
CMB has limited international presence. Embir is available worldwide on the web, with early community density growing city by city.
